structuredContent.result:
[{"name_path": "Tensor/schedule_with_vars", "kind": "Method", "relative_path": "tinygrad/tensor.py", "body_location": {"start_line": 257, "end_line": 268}, "body": "def schedule_with_vars(self, *lst:Tensor) -> tuple[list[ExecItem], dict[str, int]]:
    """
    Creates the schedule needed to realize these Tensor(s), with Variables.

    NOTE: A Tensor can only be scheduled once.
    """
    big_sink, becomes_map = transform_to_call(UOp.sink(*[x.uop for x in (self,)+lst]))
    _apply_map_to_tensors(becomes_map, name="buffers")

    # this is where the schedule cache should go
    schedule, var_vals = complete_create_schedule_with_vars(big_sink)
    return schedule, var_vals"}]

content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"run_schedule\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"tinygrad/engine/realize.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 191, \"end_line\": 211}, \"body\": \"def run_schedule(schedule:list[ExecItem], var_vals:dict[str, int]|None=None, do_update_stats=True):\\n  while len(schedule):\\n    ei = schedule.pop(0).lower()\\n    if len(capturing) and CAPTURING: capturing[0].add(ei)\\n    if VALIDATE_WITH_CPU and ei.ast.op is Ops.SINK:\\n      # copy in allocated buffers from the GPU\\n      bufs = [b for b in ei.bufs if b is not None]\\n      nb: list[Buffer|None] = [Buffer(\\\"CPU\\\", b.size, b.dtype) for b in bufs]\\n      for cpu_b, gpu_b in zip(nb, bufs):\\n        if cpu_b is not None and gpu_b.is_allocated(): cpu_b.ensure_allocated().copyin(gpu_b.as_memoryview())\\n\\n      # run on GPU\\n      ei.run(var_vals, do_update_stats=do_update_stats)\\n\\n      # validate the output buffers match (NOTE: this is assuming the output is buffer 0)\\n      with Context(BEAM=0): ExecItem(ei.ast, nb, ei.metadata, ei.fixedvars).run(var_vals, do_update_stats=do_update_stats)\\n      import numpy as np\\n      assert nb[0] is not None\\n      np.testing.assert_allclose(bufs[0].numpy(), nb[0].numpy(), rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)\\n    else:\\n      ei.run(var_vals, do_update_stats=do_update_stats)\"}]"}

tool_use_result.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"run_schedule\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"tinygrad/engine/realize.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 191, \"end_line\": 211}, \"body\": \"def run_schedule(schedule:list[ExecItem], var_vals:dict[str, int]|None=None, do_update_stats=True):\\n  while len(schedule):\\n    ei = schedule.pop(0).lower()\\n    if len(capturing) and CAPTURING: capturing[0].add(ei)\\n    if VALIDATE_WITH_CPU and ei.ast.op is Ops.SINK:\\n      # copy in allocated buffers from the GPU\\n      bufs = [b for b in ei.bufs if b is not None]\\n      nb: list[Buffer|None] = [Buffer(\\\"CPU\\\", b.size, b.dtype) for b in bufs]\\n      for cpu_b, gpu_b in zip(nb, bufs):\\n        if cpu_b is not None and gpu_b.is_allocated(): cpu_b.ensure_allocated().copyin(gpu_b.as_memoryview())\\n\\n      # run on GPU\\n      ei.run(var_vals, do_update_stats=do_update_stats)\\n\\n      # validate the output buffers match (NOTE: this is assuming the output is buffer 0)\\n      with Context(BEAM=0): ExecItem(ei.ast, nb, ei.metadata, ei.fixedvars).run(var_vals, do_update_stats=do_update_stats)\\n      import numpy as np\\n      assert nb[0] is not None\\n      np.testing.assert_allclose(bufs[0].numpy(), nb[0].numpy(), rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)\\n    else:\\n      ei.run(var_vals, do_update_stats=do_update_stats)\"}]"}

content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"complete_create_schedule_with_vars\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"tinygrad/engine/schedule.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 137, \"end_line\": 160}, \"body\": \"@track_rewrites(lambda _,ret: f\\\"Schedule {pluralize('Kernel', len(ret[0]))}\\\")\\ndef complete_create_schedule_with_vars(big_sink:UOp) -> tuple[list[ExecItem], dict[str, int]]:\\n  # big_sink srcs are all the Tensors\\n  linear_call = graph_rewrite(big_sink, pm_schedule, name=\\\"schedule to linear\\\", enter_calls=True)\\n\\n  # this recursively resolves the linear_call and allocates buffers\\n  linear = graph_rewrite(linear_call, pm_resolve_linear_call, name=\\\"resolve linear call\\\")\\n\\n  # vars used in the schedule\\n  used_vars = set().union(*[{v.expr for v in si.src[0].variables()} for si in linear.src])\\n  # get var_vals\\n  var_vals: dict[str, int] = {}\\n  for b in big_sink.src[1:]:\\n    if b.op is Ops.BIND:\\n      nm = b.src[0].expr\\n      if nm not in used_vars: continue\\n      val = b.src[1].arg\\n      if var_vals.get(nm, val) != val: raise RuntimeError(f\\\"bind mismatch on {nm}, {var_vals[nm]} != {val}\\\")\\n      var_vals[nm] = val\\n\\n  # convert LINEAR to ExecItems\\n  schedule: list[ExecItem] = linear_to_schedule(linear)\\n  with cpu_profile(TracingKey(\\\"memory planner\\\")): schedule = memory_planner(schedule)\\n  return schedule, var_vals\"}]"}

tool_use_result.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"complete_create_schedule_with_vars\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"tinygrad/engine/schedule.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 137, \"end_line\": 160}, \"body\": \"@track_rewrites(lambda _,ret: f\\\"Schedule {pluralize('Kernel', len(ret[0]))}\\\")\\ndef complete_create_schedule_with_vars(big_sink:UOp) -> tuple[list[ExecItem], dict[str, int]]:\\n  # big_sink srcs are all the Tensors\\n  linear_call = graph_rewrite(big_sink, pm_schedule, name=\\\"schedule to linear\\\", enter_calls=True)\\n\\n  # this recursively resolves the linear_call and allocates buffers\\n  linear = graph_rewrite(linear_call, pm_resolve_linear_call, name=\\\"resolve linear call\\\")\\n\\n  # vars used in the schedule\\n  used_vars = set().union(*[{v.expr for v in si.src[0].variables()} for si in linear.src])\\n  # get var_vals\\n  var_vals: dict[str, int] = {}\\n  for b in big_sink.src[1:]:\\n    if b.op is Ops.BIND:\\n      nm = b.src[0].expr\\n      if nm not in used_vars: continue\\n      val = b.src[1].arg\\n      if var_vals.get(nm, val) != val: raise RuntimeError(f\\\"bind mismatch on {nm}, {var_vals[nm]} != {val}\\\")\\n      var_vals[nm] = val\\n\\n  # convert LINEAR to ExecItems\\n  schedule: list[ExecItem] = linear_to_schedule(linear)\\n  with cpu_profile(TracingKey(\\\"memory planner\\\")): schedule = memory_planner(schedule)\\n  return schedule, var_vals\"}]"}

structuredContent.result:
[{"name_path": "complete_create_schedule_with_vars", "kind": "Function", "relative_path": "tinygrad/engine/schedule.py", "body_location": {"start_line": 137, "end_line": 160}, "body": "@track_rewrites(lambda _,ret: f"Schedule {pluralize('Kernel', len(ret[0]))}")
def complete_create_schedule_with_vars(big_sink:UOp) -> tuple[list[ExecItem], dict[str, int]]:
  # big_sink srcs are all the Tensors
  linear_call = graph_rewrite(big_sink, pm_schedule, name="schedule to linear", enter_calls=True)

  # this recursively resolves the linear_call and allocates buffers
  linear = graph_rewrite(linear_call, pm_resolve_linear_call, name="resolve linear call")

  # vars used in the schedule
  used_vars = set().union(*[{v.expr for v in si.src[0].variables()} for si in linear.src])
  # get var_vals
  var_vals: dict[str, int] = {}
  for b in big_sink.src[1:]:
    if b.op is Ops.BIND:
      nm = b.src[0].expr
      if nm not in used_vars: continue
      val = b.src[1].arg
      if var_vals.get(nm, val) != val: raise RuntimeError(f"bind mismatch on {nm}, {var_vals[nm]} != {val}")
      var_vals[nm] = val

  # convert LINEAR to ExecItems
  schedule: list[ExecItem] = linear_to_schedule(linear)
  with cpu_profile(TracingKey("memory planner")): schedule = memory_planner(schedule)
  return schedule, var_vals"}]

 var_vals = self.fixedvars if _var_vals is None else (_var_vals|self.fixedvars)\\n    # reorder bufs to match program globals if needed\\n    _bufs = [self.bufs[i] for i in self.prg.p.globals] if isinstance(self.prg, CompiledRunner) else self.bufs\\n    bufs = [unwrap(x) for x in _bufs] if jit else [unwrap(x).ensure_allocated() for x in _bufs]\\n    if PROFILE:\\n      payload = {\\\"metadata\\\":self.metadata, \\\"var_vals\\\":var_vals, \\\"bufs\\\":[b.trace_num for b in bufs], \\\"name\\\":self.prg.display_name}\\n      payload[\\\"outputs\\\"], payload[\\\"inputs\\\"] = (self.prg.p.outs, self.prg.p.ins) if isinstance(self.prg, CompiledRunner) else ([0], [1])\\n      cpu_events.append(ProfilePointEvent(self.prg.device, \\\"exec\\\", len(cpu_events), payload))\\n    et = self.prg(bufs, var_vals, wait=wait or DEBUG >= 2)\\n    if do_update_stats:\\n      GlobalCounters.kernel_count += 1\\n      GlobalCounters.global_ops += (op_est:=sym_infer(self.prg.estimates.ops, var_vals))\\n      GlobalCounters.global_mem += (mem_est:=sym_infer(self.prg.estimates.mem, var_vals))\\n      if et is not None: GlobalCounters.time_sum_s += et\\n      if DEBUG >= 2:\\n        lds_est = sym_infer(self.prg.estimates.lds, var_vals)\\n        header_color = 'magenta' if jit else ('green' if self.prg.first_run else None)\\n        ptm = colored(time_to_str(et, w=9), \\\"yellow\\\" if et > 0.01 else None) if et is not None else \\\"\\\"\\n        flops, membw, ldsbw = op_est/(et or 1e-20), mem_est/(et or 1e-20), lds_est/(et or 1e-20)\\n        flops_str = f\\\"{flops*1e-9:7.0f} GFLOPS\\\" if flops < 1e14 else colored(f\\\"{flops*1e-12:7.0f} TFLOPS\\\", 'green')\\n        mem_str = f\\\"{membw*1e-9:4.0f}|{ldsbw*1e-9:<6.0f} GB/s\\\" if membw < 1e13 and ldsbw < 1e15 else \\\\\\n          colored(f\\\"{membw*1e-12:4.0f}|{ldsbw*1e-12:<6.0f} TB/s\\\", 'green')\\n        print(f\\\"{colored(f'*** {self.prg.device[:7]:7s} {GlobalCounters.kernel_count:4d}', header_color)}\\\"+\\n          f\\\" {self.prg.display_name+' '*(46-ansilen(self.prg.display_name))} arg {len(bufs):2d} mem {GlobalCounters.mem_used/1e9:6.2f} GB\\\"+\\n          (\\\"\\\" if et is None else f\\\" tm {ptm}/{GlobalCounters.time_sum_s*1e3:9.2f}ms ({flops_str} {mem_str})\\\")+\\n          f\\\" {[repr(m) if TRACEMETA >= 2 else str(m) for m in self.metadata] if self.metadata else ''}\\\")\\n      self.prg.first_run = False\\n    return et\"}]"}

structuredContent.result:
[{"name_path": "ExecItem/run", "kind": "Method", "relative_path": "tinygrad/engine/realize.py", "body_location": {"start_line": 155, "end_line": 185}, "body": "def run(self, _var_vals:dict[str, int]|None=None, wait=False, jit=False, do_update_stats=True) -> float|None:
    if self.prg is None: self.lower()
    assert self.prg is not None
    var_vals = self.fixedvars if _var_vals is None else (_var_vals|self.fixedvars)
    # reorder bufs to match program globals if needed
    _bufs = [self.bufs[i] for i in self.prg.p.globals] if isinstance(self.prg, CompiledRunner) else self.bufs
    bufs = [unwrap(x) for x in _bufs] if jit else [unwrap(x).ensure_allocated() for x in _bufs]
    if PROFILE:
      payload = {"metadata":self.metadata, "var_vals":var_vals, "bufs":[b.trace_num for b in bufs], "name":self.prg.display_name}
      payload["outputs"], payload["inputs"] = (self.prg.p.outs, self.prg.p.ins) if isinstance(self.prg, CompiledRunner) else ([0], [1])
      cpu_events.append(ProfilePointEvent(self.prg.device, "exec", len(cpu_events), payload))
    et = self.prg(bufs, var_vals, wait=wait or DEBUG >= 2)
    if do_update_stats:
      GlobalCounters.kernel_count += 1
      GlobalCounters.global_ops += (op_est:=sym_infer(self.prg.estimates.ops, var_vals))
      GlobalCounters.global_mem += (mem_est:=sym_infer(self.prg.estimates.mem, var_vals))
      if et is not None: GlobalCounters.time_sum_s += et
      if DEBUG >= 2:
        lds_est = sym_infer(self.prg.estimates.lds, var_vals)
        header_color = 'magenta' if jit else ('green' if self.prg.first_run else None)
        ptm = colored(time_to_str(et, w=9), "yellow" if et > 0.01 else None) if et is not None else ""
        flops, membw, ldsbw = op_est/(et or 1e-20), mem_est/(et or 1e-20), lds_est/(et or 1e-20)
        flops_str = f"{flops*1e-9:7.0f} GFLOPS" if flops < 1e14 else colored(f"{flops*1e-12:7.0f} TFLOPS", 'green')
        mem_str = f"{membw*1e-9:4.0f}|{ldsbw*1e-9:<6.0f} GB/s" if membw < 1e13 and ldsbw < 1e15 else \
          colored(f"{membw*1e-12:4.0f}|{ldsbw*1e-12:<6.0f} TB/s", 'green')
        print(f"{colored(f'*** {self.prg.device[:7]:7s} {GlobalCounters.kernel_count:4d}', header_color)}"+
          f" {self.prg.display_name+' '*(46-ansilen(self.prg.display_name))} arg {len(bufs):2d} mem {GlobalCounters.mem_used/1e9:6.2f} GB"+
          ("" if et is None else f" tm {ptm}/{GlobalCounters.time_sum_s*1e3:9.2f}ms ({flops_str} {mem_str})")+
          f" {[repr(m) if TRACEMETA >= 2 else str(m) for m in self.metadata] if self.metadata else ''}")
      self.prg.first_run = False
    return et"}]

structuredContent.result:
[{"name_path": "ExecItem/lower", "kind": "Method", "relative_path": "tinygrad/engine/realize.py", "body_location": {"start_line": 143, "end_line": 153}, "body": "def lower(self):
    """Populate self.prg by lowering the AST."""
    if self.prg is not None: return self
    try: self.prg = cast(Runner, si_lowerer.rewrite(self.ast, self.bufs))
    except Exception as e:
      if DEBUG >= 2:
        print(f"error lowering {self.ast.op}")
        print("tensor operations:")
        pprint.pprint(self.metadata, indent=2)
      raise e
    return self"}]

content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"CompiledRunner/__call__\", \"kind\": \"Method\", \"relative_path\": \"tinygrad/engine/realize.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 52, \"end_line\": 60}, \"body\": \"def __call__(self, rawbufs:list[Buffer], var_vals:dict[str, int]|None=None, wait=False, timeout:int|None=None) -> float|None:\\n    if var_vals is None: var_vals = {}\\n    global_size, local_size = self.p.launch_dims(var_vals)\\n    if Device[self.p.device].renderer.has_local and local_size is None and all_int(self.p.global_size):\\n      local_size = optimize_local_size(self._prg, global_size, rawbufs)\\n      global_size = [g//l if g%l == 0 else g/l for g,l in zip(global_size, local_size)]\\n      self.p = replace(self.p, global_size=global_size, local_size=local_size)\\n    return self._prg(*[x._buf for x in rawbufs], global_size=tuple(global_size), local_size=tuple(local_size) if local_size else None,\\n                     vals=tuple(var_vals[k.expr] if k.expr not in self.p.runtimevars else None for k in self.p.vars), wait=wait, timeout=timeout)\"}]"}

[{"name_path": "transform_to_call", "kind": "Function", "relative_path": "tinygrad/engine/allocations.py", "body_location": {"start_line": 168, "end_line": 187}, "body": "@track_rewrites(lambda _,ret: f"Process {pluralize('Buffer', len(ret[1]))}")
def transform_to_call(big_sink:UOp) -> tuple[UOp, dict[UOp, UOp]]:
  if VIZ: graph_rewrite(big_sink, PatternMatcher([]), name="View Tensor Graph")
  # uop list is a list in the original_sink graph and we can map to the tags later
  # here we build buffer map
  dont_realize = {Ops.CONST, Ops.BUFFER, Ops.BIND, Ops.DEFINE_VAR, Ops.AFTER}
  ctx = AllocCtx(bases=set([x.multibase for x in big_sink.src if x.base.op not in dont_realize]))

  # this rewrite is "read-only", it adds simple things to buffer_map and may sink things on big_sink, bottom_up
  # this is the only one where we have to be careful to not break the tensor graph
  big_sink = graph_rewrite(big_sink, add_tags, ctx=ctx, bottom_up=True, name="number the uops")

  # here we can break the tensor graph. this is the only place you need to maintain numbered tags
  big_sink = graph_rewrite(big_sink, pm_early_transform_tensor_graph, ctx={}, name="early transform tensor graph")

  # here we construct the final buffer_map. this is everything that will go into the tensor map
  graph_rewrite(big_sink, pm_finalize_call, ctx=ctx, name="finalize call")
  ret = graph_rewrite(UOp.sink(*ctx.assigns), pm_replace_buf, ctx=ctx, bottom_up=True, name="replace bufs").call(*ctx.replacements)
  if VIZ: graph_rewrite(ret, PatternMatcher([]), name="View Call")
  return ret, ctx.buffer_map"}]

content.content: {"result":"[{\"name_path\": \"linear_to_schedule\", \"kind\": \"Function\", \"relative_path\": \"tinygrad/engine/schedule.py\", \"body_location\": {\"start_line\": 63, \"end_line\": 82}, \"body\": \"def linear_to_schedule(linear:UOp) -> list[ExecItem]:\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"Convert a LINEAR UOp to a list of ExecItems.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n  schedule: list[ExecItem] = []\\n  for si in linear.src:\\n    ast, buf_uops = si.src[0], si.src[1:]\\n    # create subbuffers if needed\\n    if ast.op is Ops.BUFFER_VIEW:\\n      base = buf_uops[1].buffer\\n      assert isinstance(base, Buffer), \\\"base can't be MultiBuffer\\\"\\n      buffers[buf_uops[0]] = base.view(buf_uops[0].arg, ast.dtype, ast.arg[1]*base.dtype.itemsize)\\n    ubufs = [b.buffer for b in buf_uops]\\n    metadata = si.arg.metadata\\n    if any(isinstance(x, MultiBuffer) for x in ubufs):\\n      assert all(isinstance(x, MultiBuffer) for x in ubufs), \\\"kernel must all be multibuffer\\\"\\n      dnums = [x for x in ast.variables() if x.expr == '_device_num']\\n      for j, bufs in enumerate(zip(*[x.bufs for x in cast(tuple[MultiBuffer, ...], ubufs)])):\\n        schedule.append(ExecItem(ast, list(bufs), metadata, {dnums[0].expr:j} if len(dnums) else {}))\\n    else:\\n      schedule.append(ExecItem(ast, cast(list[Buffer|None], ubufs), metadata))\\n  return schedule\"}]"}

structuredContent.result:
[{"name_path": "linear_to_schedule", "kind": "Function", "relative_path": "tinygrad/engine/schedule.py", "body_location": {"start_line": 63, "end_line": 82}, "body": "def linear_to_schedule(linear:UOp) -> list[ExecItem]:
  """Convert a LINEAR UOp to a list of ExecItems."""
  schedule: list[ExecItem] = []
  for si in linear.src:
    ast, buf_uops = si.src[0], si.src[1:]
    # create subbuffers if needed
    if ast.op is Ops.BUFFER_VIEW:
      base = buf_uops[1].buffer
      assert isinstance(base, Buffer), "base can't be MultiBuffer"
      buffers[buf_uops[0]] = base.view(buf_uops[0].arg, ast.dtype, ast.arg[1]*base.dtype.itemsize)
    ubufs = [b.buffer for b in buf_uops]
    metadata = si.arg.metadata
    if any(isinstance(x, MultiBuffer) for x in ubufs):
      assert all(isinstance(x, MultiBuffer) for x in ubufs), "kernel must all be multibuffer"
      dnums = [x for x in ast.variables() if x.expr == '_device_num']
      for j, bufs in enumerate(zip(*[x.bufs for x in cast(tuple[MultiBuffer, ...], ubufs)])):
        schedule.append(ExecItem(ast, list(bufs), metadata, {dnums[0].expr:j} if len(dnums) else {}))
    else:
      schedule.append(ExecItem(ast, cast(list[Buffer|None], ubufs), metadata))
  return schedule"}]

[{"name_path": "get_runner", "kind": "Function", "relative_path": "tinygrad/engine/realize.py", "body_location": {"start_line": 110, "end_line": 121}, "body": "def get_runner(device:str, ast:UOp) -> CompiledRunner:
  # TODO: this should be all context relevant to rendering
  context = (BEAM.value, NOOPT.value, DEVECTORIZE.value, EMULATED_DTYPES.value)
  ckey = (device, type(Device[device].compiler), ast.key, context, False)
  if cret:=method_cache.get(ckey): return cret
  bkey = (device.split(":")[0], type(Device[device].compiler), ast.key, context, True)
  if bret:=method_cache.get(bkey):
    method_cache[ckey] = ret = CompiledRunner(replace(bret.p, device=device))
  else:
    prg: ProgramSpec = get_program(ast, Device[device].renderer)
    method_cache[ckey] = method_cache[bkey] = ret = CompiledRunner(replace(prg, device=device))
  return ret"}]

May trigger BEAM search.\\n\\n  Args:\\n    ast: The Ops.SINK rooted AST\\n    renderer: The renderer used to generate the code\\n\\n  Returns:\\n    The ProgramSpec of the program.\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n  if ast.op is Ops.PROGRAM: prg = ast\\n  elif ast.op is Ops.SINK:\\n    # rewrite to prg\\n    assert isinstance(ast.arg, KernelInfo), \\\"requires KernelInfo on arg to get_program\\\"\\n    if opts is not None:\\n      # TODO: should this be here?\\n      assert ast.arg.opts_to_apply is None, \\\"can't apply opts if there's already opts to apply\\\"\\n      ast = ast.replace(arg=replace(ast.arg, opts_to_apply=tuple(opts)))\\n    full_sink = full_rewrite_to_sink(ast, renderer, optimize=ast.tag is None)\\n    prg = UOp(Ops.PROGRAM, src=(full_sink, UOp(Ops.DEVICE, arg=renderer.device)))\\n  else:\\n    raise RuntimeError(f\\\"can't call get_program on {ast.op}\\\")\\n\\n  prg = graph_rewrite(prg, pm_to_program, ctx=renderer, name=\\\"linearize/render\\\")\\n  if VIZ: graph_rewrite(prg, PatternMatcher([]), name=\\\"View Program\\\")\\n\\n  # create the ProgramSpec\\n  return ProgramSpec.from_uop(prg)\"}]"}

May trigger BEAM search.\\n\\n  Args:\\n    ast: The Ops.SINK rooted AST\\n    renderer: The renderer used to generate the code\\n\\n  Returns:\\n    The ProgramSpec of the program.\\n  \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n  if ast.op is Ops.PROGRAM: prg = ast\\n  elif ast.op is Ops.SINK:\\n    # rewrite to prg\\n    assert isinstance(ast.arg, KernelInfo), \\\"requires KernelInfo on arg to get_program\\\"\\n    if opts is not None:\\n      # TODO: should this be here?\\n      assert ast.arg.opts_to_apply is None, \\\"can't apply opts if there's already opts to apply\\\"\\n      ast = ast.replace(arg=replace(ast.arg, opts_to_apply=tuple(opts)))\\n    full_sink = full_rewrite_to_sink(ast, renderer, optimize=ast.tag is None)\\n    prg = UOp(Ops.PROGRAM, src=(full_sink, UOp(Ops.DEVICE, arg=renderer.device)))\\n  else:\\n    raise RuntimeError(f\\\"can't call get_program on {ast.op}\\\")\\n\\n  prg = graph_rewrite(prg, pm_to_program, ctx=renderer, name=\\\"linearize/render\\\")\\n  if VIZ: graph_rewrite(prg, PatternMatcher([]), name=\\\"View Program\\\")\\n\\n  # create the ProgramSpec\\n  return ProgramSpec.from_uop(prg)\"}]"}

[{"name_path": "get_program", "kind": "Function", "relative_path": "tinygrad/codegen/__init__.py", "body_location": {"start_line": 157, "end_line": 188}, "body": "@Context(ALLOW_DEVICE_USAGE=0)
@track_rewrites(name=lambda ast,renderer,ret,**kwargs: TracingKey(ret.name, (ret.function_name, ast), ret=renderer), replay=True)
def get_program(ast:UOp, renderer:Renderer, opts:list[Opt]|None=None) -> ProgramSpec:
  """
  Transform an AST into a ProgramSpec. May trigger BEAM search.

  Args:
    ast: The Ops.SINK rooted AST
    renderer: The renderer used to generate the code

  Returns:
    The ProgramSpec of the program.
  """

  if ast.op is Ops.PROGRAM: prg = ast
  elif ast.op is Ops.SINK:
    # rewrite to prg
    assert isinstance(ast.arg, KernelInfo), "requires KernelInfo on arg to get_program"
    if opts is not None:
      # TODO: should this be here?
      assert ast.arg.opts_to_apply is None, "can't apply opts if there's already opts to apply"
      ast = ast.replace(arg=replace(ast.arg, opts_to_apply=tuple(opts)))
    full_sink = full_rewrite_to_sink(ast, renderer, optimize=ast.tag is None)
    prg = UOp(Ops.PROGRAM, src=(full_sink, UOp(Ops.DEVICE, arg=renderer.device)))
  else:
    raise RuntimeError(f"can't call get_program on {ast.op}")

  prg = graph_rewrite(prg, pm_to_program, ctx=renderer, name="linearize/render")
  if VIZ: graph_rewrite(prg, PatternMatcher([]), name="View Program")

  # create the ProgramSpec
  return ProgramSpec.from_uop(prg)"}]

content.thinking:
Now I have a complete picture of the execution path. Let me summarize it.

1. `Tensor.realize()` — `tinygrad/tensor.py`, line 276
   - Filters tensors that don't have buffer identity yet
   - Calls `Tensor.schedule_with_vars()` + `run_schedule()`

2. `Tensor.schedule_with_vars()` — `tinygrad/tensor.py`, line 257
   - Calls `transform_to_call()` (in `tinygrad/engine/allocations.py`) to preprocess the UOp graph
   - Then invokes `complete_create_schedule_with_vars()` (in `tinygrad/engine/schedule.py`)

3. `complete_create_schedule_with_vars()` — `tinygrad/engine/schedule.py`, line 137
   - Applies graph rewrites to linearize the computation, then converts it into a list of executable items and runs memory planning on the result

4. `run_schedule()` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`
   - Iterates through each executable item, compiling it via `lower()` and then executing it with `run()`

5. `ExecItem.lower()` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`, line 143
   - Uses pattern matching to transform the item into a `Runner`, which internally invokes `get_program()` to compile the AST

6. `get_runner()` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`, line 110
   - Checks a cache, then calls `get_program()` to compile the AST into a `ProgramSpec`, and wraps it in a `CompiledRunner`

7. `get_program()`
   - Optimizes the AST, applies linearization and rendering rewrites, and returns the compiled `ProgramSpec`

8. `CompiledRunner.__init__()` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`, line 38
   - Compiles the program source for the target device and creates a runtime handle to the compiled program

9. `CompiledRunner.__call__()` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`, line 52
   - Invokes the device program with the buffer pointers to execute it

So the execution flow traces through tensor operations down to device dispatch, with the key modules being the tensor entry point, buffer allocation and graph transformation, lazy graph scheduling, lowering and execution, and code generation.

content.text:
Here is the complete trace of `Tensor.realize()` through tinygrad's scheduling and execution pipeline:

---

## 1. Entry Point — `tinygrad/tensor.py`, line 276

```python
def realize(self, *lst:Tensor, do_update_stats=True) -> Tensor:
```

- Collects tensors that don't yet have a buffer identity (`not x.uop.has_buffer_identity()`)
- Calls two functions in sequence:
  1. `Tensor.schedule_with_vars(*to_realize)` — builds the schedule
  2. `run_schedule(schedule, var_vals, ...)` — executes it

---

## 2. Scheduling Path

### `Tensor.schedule_with_vars` — `tinygrad/tensor.py`, line 257
- Calls **`transform_to_call(UOp.sink(...))`** from `tinygrad/engine/allocations.py` (line 168)
  - Runs several `graph_rewrite` passes over the lazy UOp graph to number UOps, assign buffers, and finalize the "call" structure
  - Returns `(big_sink, becomes_map)` — the transformed graph and a buffer-mapping dict
- Calls **`complete_create_schedule_with_vars(big_sink)`** from `tinygrad/engine/schedule.py`

### `complete_create_schedule_with_vars` — `tinygrad/engine/schedule.py`, line 137
- Applies `graph_rewrite(big_sink, pm_schedule, ...)` — rewrites lazy ops into a "linear call" graph (all kernel boundaries are resolved here via pattern matching)
- Applies `graph_rewrite(linear_call, pm_resolve_linear_call, ...)` — resolves and allocates buffers
- Calls **`linear_to_schedule(linear)`** (line 63, same file) — converts each `LINEAR` UOp into an `ExecItem` (containing an AST and buffer list)
- Calls **`memory_planner(schedule)`** — performs buffer aliasing/reuse optimizations
- Returns `(list[ExecItem], dict[str, int])` — the schedule and variable bindings

---

## 3. Execution Path

### `run_schedule` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`, line 191
- Iterates the `ExecItem` list:
  1. Calls **`ei.lower()`** — compiles the AST into a runnable program
  2. Calls **`ei.run(var_vals, ...)`** — dispatches it to the device

### `ExecItem.lower` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`, line 143
- Uses `si_lowerer.rewrite(self.ast, self.bufs)` — a `PatternMatcher` (defined at line 126) that routes SINK ASTs to **`get_runner(device, ast)`** (line 111, same file)

### `get_runner` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`, line 110
- Checks a `method_cache` for previously compiled programs
- On cache miss, calls **`get_program(ast, Device[device].renderer)`** from `tinygrad/codegen/__init__.py` to compile
- Wraps the result in a **`CompiledRunner`** (line 38, same file)

### `get_program` — `tinygrad/codegen/__init__.py`, line 157
- Calls `full_rewrite_to_sink(ast, renderer, ...)` — applies optimization passes (loop tiling, vectorization, BEAM search if enabled)
- Applies `graph_rewrite(prg, pm_to_program, ...)` — the "linearize/render" pass that converts the optimized AST into source code
- Returns a **`ProgramSpec`** containing the source string and metadata

### `CompiledRunner.__init__` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`, line 38
- Calls `Device[p.device].compiler.compile_cached(p.src)` — invokes the device-specific compiler (e.g., OpenCL/CUDA/Metal) to produce a binary `lib`
- Creates `self._prg = Device[p.device].runtime(p.function_name, self.p.lib, ...)` — the actual device program handle

### `CompiledRunner.__call__` — `tinygrad/engine/realize.py`, line 52
- Resolves `global_size` / `local_size` from the `ProgramSpec`
- Calls `self._prg(*[x._buf for x in rawbufs], global_size=..., local_size=..., vals=...)` — the final dispatch to the device runtime

---

## Summary of Files Involved

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `tinygrad/tensor.py` | Entry point — `Tensor.realize` and `Tensor.schedule_with_vars` |
| `tinygrad/engine/allocations.py` | `transform_to_call` — UOp graph transformation and buffer assignment |
| `tinygrad/engine/schedule.py` | `complete_create_schedule_with_vars`, `linear_to_schedule`, `memory_planner` — full lazy graph → `ExecItem` list |
| `tinygrad/engine/realize.py` | `run_schedule`, `ExecItem.lower/run`, `get_runner`, `CompiledRunner` — compilation cache + device dispatch |
| `tinygrad/codegen/__init__.py` | `get_program` — AST optimization, linearization, and source code rendering |
